_comment: All game constants live here. No magic numbers in code.

rating._comment: magnitude = base * (mag_floor + mag_per_star * stars); conversion = conv_base + conv_per_star * stars

feed._comment: poll_seconds 300 = one 5-minute poll per window. Only the lead client triggers upstream fetches (see relay cache_ttl + client.lead_username), so a full half is ~10 refreshes x 2 calls + 1 lineups = ~21 queries/game; 3 games < 80/day. follower_poll_delay_seconds offsets non-lead polls so the lead's fresh write lands in the cache before followers read it.

client._comment: The lead client (username match is case-insensitive) is the ONLY client that triggers upstream API-Football fetches; all other clients read the shared website cache. This bounds the day's quota to one client's poll rate no matter how many people join.

live._comment: fixture_id is the single-game default for --live; fixtures seeds the live feed kickoff for the curated set. The PICKER now reads assets/data/schedule.json (live.schedule) instead of fixtures. Free tier = 100 req/day; relay caches per fixture, fetches lineups once. warn_minutes_before: show a 'too early, come back later' notice when kickoff is further off than this. start_minutes_before: the lead does not poll the API until kickoff is within this many minutes (saves quota); a kickoff countdown shows meanwhile.

schedule._comment: Picker source. file = assets/data basename. lookahead_hours/drop_after_hours bound the rolling window; live_window_minutes is the generous span a game is treated as live (2 halves + stoppage + HT) since the viewer does not poll the API; soon_minutes is the 'soon' threshold.

launcher._comment: Web entry menu: Live match (real fixtures + relay) vs an offline simulated test game. test_sim is a bundled recorded match (no API, no waiting) for trying the full flow.
